mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 9.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and earnings downgrade expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts signals a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate profit margins and reduces equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a statistical significance exceeding p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate resilient household spending that offsets tight financial conditions, supporting positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to exhibit a positive correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over a one-month horizon as corporate cash flows rise.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 jobs signals robust labor demand that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 200,000 fails to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with declining industrial production indicate tightening financial conditions are actively constraining real economic activity, leading to negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO does not yield a negative predictive relationship with 20-day forward SPX returns exceeding a Rank IC of -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, rising residential housing starts act as a leading indicator of economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon as construction activity stimulates broader demand.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns when UNRATE remains above 8.5 percent, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year window.
